steps.title: Identify corresponding sides from the vertex correspondence
steps.content: Since P corresponds to S and Q corresponds to T, side $\overline{PQ}$ corresponds to side $\overline{ST}$. Since Q corresponds to T and R corresponds to U, side $\overline{QR}$ corresponds to side $\overline{TU}$.

steps.title: Calculate the scale factor
steps.content:
For similar triangles, the ratio of corresponding sides is constant. Find the scale factor from triangle PQR to triangle STU using the known corresponding sides:
$$\text{Scale factor} = \frac{ST}{PQ} = \frac{8}{6} = \frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the scale factor to find TU
steps.content:
Since $\overline{QR}$ and $\overline{TU}$ are corresponding sides, they must have the same ratio as all other corresponding sides:
$$\frac{TU}{QR} = \frac{4}{3}$$
$$TU = 24 \times \frac{4}{3} = \frac{96}{3} = 32$$
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
